examples.title: Basic HTTP Request-Response Pattern
examples.description: Demonstrates how web applications handle client requests
examples.code:
// CLIENT SIDE (Browser)
FUNCTION sendRequest(url, method, data):
 REQUEST = createHTTPRequest(method, url)
 IF data EXISTS:
 REQUEST.setBody(serializeToJSON(data))
 ENDIF
 RESPONSE = network.send(REQUEST)
 RETURN parseJSON(RESPONSE.body)
END FUNCTION

// Example Usage
result = sendRequest("https://api.example.com/users", "GET", NULL)
display(result)

// SERVER SIDE
FUNCTION handleRequest(request):
 method = request.method
 path = request.url
 
 IF method == "GET" AND path == "/users":
 users = database.query("SELECT * FROM users")
 RETURN createResponse(200, toJSON(users))
 ELSE:
 RETURN createResponse(404, "Not Found")
 ENDIF
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Shows the request-response cycle: client creates HTTP request, sends to server, server processes and returns response

examples.title: Session Management for Stateless HTTP
examples.description: Demonstrates how to maintain state across stateless HTTP requests
examples.code:
// SERVER SIDE SESSION MANAGEMENT
SESSION_STORE = {} // In-memory or database

FUNCTION handleLogin(username, password):
 user = database.findUser(username)
 IF user.password == hash(password):
 sessionId = generateUniqueId()
 SESSION_STORE[sessionId] = {
 "userId": user.id,
 "username": user.username,
 "loginTime": currentTime(),
 "expiry": currentTime() + 3600 // 1 hour
 }
 RETURN createCookie("sessionId", sessionId)
 ELSE:
 RETURN createResponse(401, "Invalid credentials")
 ENDIF
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION authenticateRequest(request):
 sessionId = request.cookies["sessionId"]
 session = SESSION_STORE[sessionId]
 
 IF session == NULL OR session.expiry < currentTime():
 RETURN NULL // Not authenticated
 ELSE:
 RETURN session.userId // Return authenticated user
 ENDIF
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Demonstrates how web applications overcome HTTP's stateless nature by using session IDs stored in cookies

examples.title: Simple REST API Endpoint
examples.description: Demonstrates RESTful web service design principles
examples.code:
// RESTful API Design
// Resource: /users

FUNCTION handleAPIRequest(method, path, body, headers):
 // Route to appropriate handler
 IF path == "/users":
 SELECT method:
 CASE "GET":
 // Get all users
 users = database.query("SELECT * FROM users")
 RETURN createResponse(200, users, "application/json")
 
 CASE "POST":
 // Create new user
 newUser = parseJSON(body)
 id = database.insert("users", newUser)
 RETURN createResponse(201, {"id": id}, "application/json")
 
 ENDSELECT
 ELSEIF path MATCHES "/users/[0-9]+":
 userId = extractIdFromPath(path)
 
 SELECT method:
 CASE "GET":
 user = database.find("users", userId)
 RETURN createResponse(200, user, "application/json")
 
 CASE "PUT":
 updatedUser = parseJSON(body)
 database.update("users", userId, updatedUser)
 RETURN createResponse(200, "Updated", "application/json")
 
 CASE "DELETE":
 database.delete("users", userId)
 RETURN createResponse(204, NULL, "application/json")
 ENDSELECT
 ENDIF
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Shows RESTful design: proper HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE), resource-based URLs, JSON responses

examples.title: AJAX Call for Asynchronous Update
examples.description: Demonstrates how AJAX enables partial page updates without reload
examples.code:
// CLIENT-SIDE AJAX
FUNCTION loadUserProfile(userId):
 // Create XMLHttpRequest object
 xhr = new XMLHttpRequest()
 
 // Set up callback for when response arrives
 xhr.onload = FUNCTION():
 IF xhr.status == 200:
 user = JSON.parse(xhr.responseText)
 // Update only the profile section
 document.getElementById("username").innerText = user.name
 document.getElementById("email").innerText = user.email
 document.getElementById("profile").style.display = "block"
 ELSE:
 displayError("Failed to load profile")
 ENDIF
 END FUNCTION
 
 // Configure and send request
 xhr.open("GET", "/api/users/" + userId, true) // true = async
 xhr.setRequestHeader("Content-Type", "application/json")
 xhr.send()
 
 // Page continues to be interactive while loading
 displayLoadingSpinner()
END FUNCTION

// Modern fetch API equivalent
FUNCTION loadUserProfileModern(userId):
 fetch("/api/users/" + userId)
 .then(response => response.json())
 .then(user => updateProfileUI(user))
 .catch(error => displayError(error))
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: AJAX enables asynchronous communication - page doesn't reload, only specific sections update, UI remains responsive
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for UI update, O(n) for network
